verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with a large extracellular domain and a short cytoplasmic C-tail. In resting/acrosome-intact sperm, it is sequestered inside the acrosomal compartment and not surface-accessible. Following capacitation and acrosomal exocytosis (the acrosome reaction), IZUMO1 is translocated to the equatorial/post-acrosomal segment of the sperm plasma membrane, where its ectodomain is fully exposed on the outer leaflet and engages its egg receptor JUNO. Surface exposure is therefore strictly cell-state dependent (acrosome reaction). Because the protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM anchor during the surface state, the verdict is contextual/cell_state_induced rather than no.
